transcription:
C-L is decentralized
Snapshots are for

- Checkpointing: Take periodic snapshots to
return to in case of failure

- Stable property detection (if it's in the
snapshot, it's true in the current run/now)

Stable property: Once it's true, stays true (deadlock,
termination)
A good snapshot algo will make a consistent
cut which divides the past and future
of a system with happens-before relations
Reliable delivery
idea- keep sending until you get an ack

Lecture #8
- Reg Fault Taxonomy.

Omission Fault: A message is lost
Crash Fault: A process fails by halting
Timing Fault: Something happens too late or too early
Byzantine Fault: A process behaves in an arbitrary or
malicious way.
